nodes.label: Effective Amount Calculation Pattern
nodes.rationale: Jobs without a manual override on Jobs.amount use a calculated effectiveAmount derived from jobType.rateOfJob × dispatch inputs (loads, hours, weight). This is the single source of truth for amount computation server-side, mirroring computeJobPreviewAmount in the UI. NULL rate or rate <= 0 means rate-pending, matching TypeScript computeJobAmountFromInputs behavior.

nodes.label: Rate Pending Job Type Pattern
nodes.rationale: rateOfJob made nullable in JobTypes so that NULL means rate-pending (not yet assigned) vs 0 which means free. This enables the system to distinguish pending pricing from intentionally zero-rate jobs.

nodes.label: UTC Time Standardization Migration
nodes.rationale: startTime/endTime columns in Jobs were stored as NVARCHAR with mixed formats (Eastern Time). Migration converts to DATETIME2 stored in UTC using AT TIME ZONE conversion. Old columns dropped, new DATETIME2 columns renamed to replace them.

nodes.label: Invoice Amount Override Pattern
nodes.rationale: JobInvoice.amount made nullable to become override-only. NULL means inherit the underlying job effectiveAmount via vJobInvoiceEffective view. Non-NULL is a manual per-invoice override (e.g. negotiated discount). This means invoice totals track current job-type rates automatically without requiring backfills.

nodes.label: Agent Response Quality Issues
nodes.rationale: Known issues include: verbose/unformatted responses, company vs dispatcher confusion, not searching before confirming, job type not searched by route, raw tool results shown to user, multi-image batches mixing up fields between tickets.

nodes.label: Company vs Dispatcher Entity Distinction
nodes.rationale: Company (companyId) is the client whose name/logo appears on the ticket letterhead - the work site owner. Dispatcher (dispatcherId) is the trucking/dispatch company that assigned the truck and driver. These are different entities and must never be mixed up. Ticket letterhead = Company; Trucking Co field = Dispatcher.

nodes.label: Job Type Naming Convention
nodes.rationale: Job type names follow [Company] - [Route/Location] - [Rate Type] pattern, e.g. 'Birnam - Strathroy - Hourly - 105'. This encodes routing and billing structure in the title for easy identification.
